revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''カミアシャギ'''(神アシャギ、神アサギ)は[[沖縄本島]]北部地方で、集落の神祭りを行う場所をいう。茅ぶき屋根…」

revision.text:
'''カミアシャギ'''（神アシャギ、神アサギ）は、[[沖縄本島]]北部地方において、集落の神祭りを行う[[小屋]]。4本柱または6本柱で[[壁]]がない吹き抜け構造で、床張りもなく、軒が極端に低く、腰をかがめないと中に入れない。茅ぶき屋根の[[寄棟造]]が伝統的な様式だが、戦後には[[コンクリート]]造りのものも登場した<ref name=shimpo>[https://ryukyushimpo.jp/okinawa-dic/prentry-40953.html 最新版沖縄コンパクト事典]</ref>。
カミアシャギは沖縄本島中南部に見られる祭祀場のトゥン（殿）の古い形態と言われ<ref name=shimpo/><ref>高橋恵子『沖縄の御願ことば辞典』ボーダーインク、p. 70</ref>、カミアシャギが分布する地域は「神アサギ文化圏」「北山文化圏」に分類され、三山統一以前の[[北山王国]]の版図に重なるとされる<ref>高橋哲朗「沖縄の伝統行事 芸能を歩く」沖縄探見社、p. 59</ref>。
== 文化財 == 以下のカミアシャギは、沖縄県の民俗有形文化財に指定されている。 * [[伊平屋村]]我喜屋の神あしあげ 1棟 - [[1977年]]（[[昭和]]52年）[[7月11日]]指定<ref name=list>[http://www.pref.okinawa.jp/edu/bunkazai/edu/jimukyoku/bunkazai/documents/h25_012.pdf 沖縄県教育庁文化財課要覧（平成28年度版） 国・県・市町村指定文化財]（PDF）、2018年2月8日閲覧。</ref> * 伊平屋村島尻の神あしあげ 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/> * [[伊是名村]]諸見の神アサギ附拝所 1棟 - [[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）[[2月4日]]指定<ref name=list/> * 伊是名村仲田の神アサギ附宅地 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/> * 伊是名村伊是名の神アサギ附拝所 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/> * 伊是名村勢理客の神アサギ附宅地 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/>
==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{デフォルトソート:かみあしやき}} [[Category:沖縄県の宗教施設|*かみあしやき]] [[Category:琉球の信仰]]

'''カミアシャギ'''（神アシャギ、神アサギ）は、[[沖縄本島]]北部地方において、集落の神祭りを行う[[小屋]]。4本柱または6本柱で[[壁]]がない吹き抜け構造で、床張りもなく、軒が極端に低く、腰をかがめないと中に入れない。[[茅葺]][[屋根]]の[[寄棟造]]が伝統的な様式だが、戦後には[[コンクリート]]造りのものも登場した<ref name=shimpo>[https://ryukyushimpo.jp/okinawa-dic/prentry-40953.html 最新版沖縄コンパクト事典]</ref>。
[[File:Kami-Asagi.jpg|thumb|カミアシャギ|250px]]

== 文化財 == 以下のカミアシャギは、沖縄県の[[有形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。 * [[伊平屋村]]我喜屋の神あしあげ 1棟 - 1977年（昭和52年）7月11日指定<ref name=list>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.okinawa.jp/edu/bunkazai/edu/jimukyoku/bunkazai/documents/h25_012.pdf |title=沖縄県教育庁文化財課要覧（平成28年度版） 国・県・市町村指定文化財|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-02-08}}</ref> * 伊平屋村島尻の神あしあげ 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/> * [[伊是名村]]諸見の神アサギ附拝所 1棟 - 1994年（平成6年）2月4日指定<ref name=list/> * 伊是名村仲田の神アサギ附宅地 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/> * 伊是名村伊是名の神アサギ附拝所 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/> * 伊是名村勢理客の神アサギ附宅地 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/>

revision.text:
'''カミアシャギ'''（神アシャギ、神アサギ）は、[[沖縄本島]]北部地方において、集落の神祭りを行う[[小屋]]。4本柱または6本柱で[[壁]]がない吹き抜け構造で、床張りもなく、軒が極端に低く、腰をかがめないと中に入れない。[[茅葺]][[屋根]]の[[寄棟造]]が伝統的な様式だが、戦後には[[コンクリート]]造りのものも登場した<ref name=shimpo>[https://ryukyushimpo.jp/okinawa-dic/prentry-40953.html 最新版沖縄コンパクト事典]</ref>。
[[File:Kami-Asagi.jpg|thumb|カミアシャギ|250px]]
==Overview== カミアシャギは沖縄本島中南部に見られる祭祀場のトゥン（殿）の古い形態と言われ<ref name=shimpo/><ref>高橋恵子『沖縄の御願ことば辞典』ボーダーインク、p.70</ref>、カミアシャギが分布する地域は「神アサギ文化圏」「北山文化圏」に分類され、三山統一以前の[[北山王国]]の版図に重なるとされる<ref>高橋哲朗「沖縄の伝統行事 芸能を歩く」沖縄探見社、p. 59</ref>。
== Cultural Properties == 以下のカミアシャギは、沖縄県の[[有形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。 * [[伊平屋村]]我喜屋の神あしあげ 1棟 - 1977年（昭和52年）7月11日指定<ref name=list>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.okinawa.jp/edu/bunkazai/edu/jimukyoku/bunkazai/documents/h25_012.pdf |title=沖縄県教育庁文化財課要覧（平成28年度版） 国・県・市町村指定文化財|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-02-08}}</ref> * 伊平屋村島尻の神あしあげ 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/> * [[伊是名村]]諸見の神アサギ附拝所 1棟 - 1994年（平成6年）2月4日指定<ref name=list/> * 伊是名村仲田の神アサギ附宅地 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/> * 伊是名村伊是名の神アサギ附拝所 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/> * 伊是名村勢理客の神アサギ附宅地 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/>

revision.text:
'''Kamiashagi''' (神アシャギ, 神アサギ) is a type of {{ill|hut|ja|小屋}} used to conduct community deity festivals in the northern region of {{ill|Okinawa Main Island|ja|沖縄本島}}. These structures are characterized by an open interior with no {{ill|wall|ja|壁}}, supported by either four or six pillars, lacking a floor structure, with extremely low eaves that require one to stoop in order to enter. The traditional style features a thatched {{ill|roof|ja|屋根}} in the form of a {{ill|hip roof|ja|寄棟造}}, though in the postwar period, concrete constructions ({{ill|concrete|ja|コンクリート}}) have also appeared.<ref name=shimpo>[https://ryukyushimpo.jp/okinawa-dic/prentry-40953.html 最新版沖縄コンパクト事典]</ref>
[[File:Kami-Asagi.jpg|thumb|Kamiashagi|250px]]
== Overview == Kamiashagi is considered an ancient form of the shrine hall (tun) used as a venue for ritual ceremonies in central-southern {{ill|Okinawa Main Island|ja|沖縄本島}}.<ref name=shimpo/><ref>高橋恵子『沖縄の御願ことば辞典』ボーダーインク, p.70</ref> The regions where Kamiashagi are found are classified as the "Kami-asagi Cultural Sphere" and the "Kitayama Cultural Sphere," and are said to overlap with the territory of the {{ill|Kitayama Kingdom|ja|北山王国}} prior to the unification of the Three Mountains.<ref>高橋哲朗「沖縄の伝統行事 芸能を歩く」沖縄探見社, p. 59</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The following Kamiashagi have been designated as {{ill|Tangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|有形民俗文化財}} of Okinawa Prefecture: * {{ill|Iheiya Village|ja|伊平屋村}} – the Kamiashagi of Gakiya, 1 structure – designated on July 11, 1977 (Showa 52)<ref name=list>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.okinawa.jp/edu/bunkazai/edu/jimukyoku/bunkazai/documents/h25_012.pdf |title=沖縄県教育庁文化財課要覧（平成28年度版） 国・県・市町村指定文化財|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-02-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Iheiya Village|ja|伊平屋村}} – the Kamiashagi of Shima-jiri, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena Village|ja|伊是名村}} – the Kamiasagi Worship Hall at Shomimi, 1 structure – designated on February 4, 1994 (Heisei 6)<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena Village|ja|伊是名村}} – the Kamiasagi annex residential site at Nakada, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena Village|ja|伊是名村}} – the Kamiasagi Worship Hall at Izena, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena Village|ja|伊是名村}} – the Kamiasagi annex residential site at Serikyaku, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/>
== Sources == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{Japanese Cultural Properties}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Okinawa Prefecture}} {{citation needed|kamiashiyaki}}
[[Category:Religious Facilities in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kami Asagi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kami Asagi]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:カミアシャギ]]
[[Category:Ryukyuan religion]] [[Category:Ryukyu Shinto]][[ja:カミアシャギ]][[Category:Ryukyu Shinto]] [[Category:Pages t]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:ted]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kami Asagi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kami Asagi]][[sn:ami Asagi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ami Asagi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kami Asagi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kami Asagi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kami Asagi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kami Asagi]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami Asagi}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami Asagi}} [[Category:New]]

'''Kamiashagi''' (神アシャギ, 神アサギ) is a type of {{ill|hut|ja|小屋}} used to conduct community deity festivals in the northern region of {{ill|Okinawa Main Island|ja|沖縄本島}}. These structures are characterized by an open interior with no {{ill|wall|ja|壁}}, supported by either four or six pillars, lacking a floor structure, with extremely low eaves that require one to stoop in order to enter. The traditional style features a thatched {{ill|roof|ja|屋根}} in the form of a {{ill|hip roof|ja|寄棟造}}, though in the postwar period, concrete constructions ({{ill|concrete|ja|コンクリート}}) have also appeared.<ref name=shimpo>[https://ryukyushimpo.jp/okinawa-dic/prentry-40953.html 最新版沖縄コンパクト事典]</ref>
[[File:Kami-Asagi.jpg|thumb|Kamiashagi|250px]]
== Overview == Kamiashagi is considered an ancient form of the shrine hall (tun) used as a venue for ritual ceremonies in central-southern {{ill|Okinawa Main Island|ja|沖縄本島}}.<ref name=shimpo/><ref>高橋恵子『沖縄の御願ことば辞典』ボーダーインク, p.70</ref> The regions where Kamiashagi are found are classified as the "Kami-asagi Cultural Sphere" and the "Kitayama Cultural Sphere," and are said to overlap with the territory of the {{ill|Kitayama Kingdom|ja|北山王国}} prior to the unification of the Three Mountains.<ref>高橋哲朗「沖縄の伝統行事 芸能を歩く」沖縄探見社, p. 59</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The following Kamiashagi have been designated as {{ill|Tangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|有形民俗文化財}} of Okinawa Prefecture: * {{ill|Iheiya Village|ja|伊平屋村}} – the Kamiashagi of Gakiya, 1 structure – designated on July 11, 1977 (Showa 52)<ref name=list>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.okinawa.jp/edu/bunkazai/edu/jimukyoku/bunkazai/documents/h25_012.pdf |title=沖縄県教育庁文化財課要覧（平成28年度版） 国・県・市町村指定文化財|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-02-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Iheiya Village|ja|伊平屋村}} – the Kamiashagi of Shima-jiri, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena Village|ja|伊是名村}} – the Kamiasagi Worship Hall at Shomimi, 1 structure – designated on February 4, 1994 (Heisei 6)<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena Village|ja|伊是名村}} – the Kamiasagi annex residential site at Nakada, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena Village|ja|伊是名村}} – the Kamiasagi Worship Hall at Izena, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena Village|ja|伊是名村}} – the Kamiasagi annex residential site at Serikyaku, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/>

These structures are characterized by an open interior with no {{ill|wall|ja|壁}}, supported by either four or six pillars, lacking a floor structure, with extremely low eaves that require one to stoop in order to enter. The traditional style features a thatched {{ill|roof|ja|屋根}} in the form of a {{ill|hip roof|ja|寄棟造}}, though in the postwar period, concrete constructions ({{ill|concrete|ja|コンクリート}}) have also appeared.<ref name=shimpo>[https://ryukyushimpo.jp/okinawa-dic/prentry-40953.html 最新版沖縄コンパクト事典]</ref>
[[File:Kami-Asagi.jpg|thumb|Kamiashagi|250px]]
== Overview == Kamiashagi is considered an ancient form of the shrine hall (tun) used as a venue for ritual ceremonies in central-southern {{ill|Okinawa Main Island|ja|沖縄本島}}.<ref name=shimpo/><ref>高橋恵子『沖縄の御願ことば辞典』ボーダーインク, p.70</ref> The regions where Kamiashagi are found are classified as the "Kami-asagi Cultural Sphere" and the "Kitayama Cultural Sphere," and are said to overlap with the territory of the {{ill|Kitayama Kingdom|ja|北山王国}} prior to the unification of the Three Mountains.<ref>高橋哲朗「沖縄の伝統行事 芸能を歩く」沖縄探見社, p. 59</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The following Kamiashagi have been designated as {{ill|Tangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|有形民俗文化財}} of Okinawa Prefecture: * {{ill|Iheiya Village|ja|伊平屋村}} – the Kamiashagi of Gakiya, 1 structure – designated on July 11, 1977 (Showa 52)<ref name=list>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.okinawa.jp/edu/bunkazai/edu/jimukyoku/bunkazai/documents/h25_012.pdf |title=沖縄県教育庁文化財課要覧（平成28th year 度版） 国・県・市町村指定文化財|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-02-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Iheiya Village|ja|伊平屋村}} – the Kamiashagi of Shima-jiri, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena Village|ja|伊是名村}} – the Kamiasagi Worship Hall at Shomimi, 1 structure – designated on February 4, 1994 (Heisei 6)<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena Village|ja|伊是名村}} – the Kamiasagi annex residential site at Nakada, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena Village|ja|伊是名村}} – the Kamiasagi Worship Hall at Izena, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena Village|ja|伊是名村}} – the Kamiasagi annex residential site at Serikyaku, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/>
== Sources ==

These structures are characterized by an open interior with no {{ill|wall|ja|壁}}, supported by either four or six pillars, lacking a floor structure, with extremely low eaves that require one to stoop in order to enter. The traditional style features a thatched {{ill|roof|ja|屋根}} in the form of a {{ill|hip roof|ja|寄棟造}}, though in the postwar period, concrete constructions ({{ill|concrete|ja|コンクリート}}) have also appeared.<ref name=shimpo>[https://ryukyushimpo.jp/okinawa-dic/prentry-40953.html 最新版沖縄コンパクト事典]</ref>
[[File:Kami-Asagi.jpg|thumb|Kamiashagi|250px]]

revision.text:
'''カミアシャギ'''（神アシャギ、神アサギ）は、[[沖縄本島]]北部地方において、集落の神祭りを行う[[小屋]]。4本柱または6本柱で[[壁]]がない吹き抜け構造で、床張りもなく、軒が極端に低く、腰をかがめないと中に入れない。[[茅葺]][[屋根]]の[[寄棟造]]が伝統的な様式だが、戦後には[[コンクリート]]造りのものも登場した<ref name=shimpo>[https://ryukyushimpo.jp/okinawa-dic/prentry-40953.html 最新版沖縄コンパクト事典]</ref>。
[[File:Kami-Asagi.jpg|thumb|カミアシャギ|250px]]
==概要== カミアシャギは沖縄本島中南部に見られる祭祀場のトゥン（殿）の古い形態と言われ<ref name=shimpo/><ref>高橋恵子『沖縄の御願ことば辞典』ボーダーインク、p.70</ref>、カミアシャギが分布する地域は「神アサギ文化圏」「北山文化圏」に分類され、三山統一以前の[[北山王国]]の版図に重なるとされる<ref>高橋哲朗「沖縄の伝統行事 芸能を歩く」沖縄探見社、p. 59</ref>。
== 文化財 == 以下のカミアシャギは、沖縄県の[[有形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。 * [[伊平屋村]]我喜屋の神あしあげ 1棟 - 1977年（昭和52年）7月11日指定<ref name=list>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.okinawa.jp/edu/bunkazai/edu/jimukyoku/bunkazai/documents/h25_012.pdf |title=沖縄県教育庁文化財課要覧（平成28年度版） 国・県・市町村指定文化財|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-02-08}}</ref> * 伊平屋村島尻の神あしあげ 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/> * [[伊是名村]]諸見の神アサギ附拝所 1棟 - 1994年（平成6年）2月4日指定<ref name=list/> * 伊是名村仲田の神アサギ附宅地 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/> * 伊是名村伊是名の神アサギ附拝所 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/> * 伊是名村勢理客の神アサギ附宅地 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/>
==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{日本の文化財}} {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{デフォルトソート:かみあしやき}} [[Category:沖縄県の宗教施設|*かみあしやき]] [[Category:琉球の信仰]]

== 文化財 == 以下のカミアシャギは、沖縄県の[[有形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。 * [[伊平屋村]]我喜屋の神あしあげ 1棟 - 1977年（昭和52年）7月11日指定<ref name=list>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.okinawa.jp/edu/bunkazai/edu/jimukyoku/bunkazai/documents/h25_012.pdf |title=沖縄県教育庁文化財課要覧（平成28年度版） 国・県・市町村指定文化財|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-02-08}}</ref> * 伊平屋村島尻の神あしあげ 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/> * [[伊是名村]]諸見の神アサギ附拝所 1棟 - 1994年（平成6年）2月4日指定<ref name=list/> * 伊是名村仲田の神アサギ附宅地 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/> * 伊是名村伊是名の神アサギ附拝所 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/> * 伊是名村勢理客の神アサギ附宅地 1棟 - 同上<ref name=list/>
==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{日本の文化財}} {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} {{デフォルトソート:かみあしやき}} [[Category:沖縄県の宗教施設|*かみあしやき]] [[Category:琉球の信仰]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カミアシャギ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48764287]] Wikidata name: kami ashagi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48764287]] Wikidata name: kami ashagi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamiashagi''' (神アシャギ, 神アサギ) is a type of [[:en:Hut|hut]] used to conduct community deity festivals in the northern region of [[:en:Okinawa Island|Okinawa Main Island]]. These structures are characterized by an open interior with no [[:en:Wall|wall]], supported by either four or six pillars, lacking a floor structure, with extremely low eaves that require one to stoop in order to enter. The traditional style features a thatched [[:en:Roof|roof]] in the form of a [[:en:Hip roof|hip roof]], though in the postwar period, concrete constructions ([[:en:Concrete|concrete]]) have also appeared.<ref name=shimpo>[https://ryukyushimpo.jp/okinawa-dic/prentry-40953.html 最新版沖縄コンパクト事典]</ref>
[[File:Kami-Asagi.jpg|thumb|Kamiashagi|250px]]
== Overview == Kamiashagi is considered an ancient form of the shrine hall (tun) used as a venue for ritual ceremonies in central-southern [[:en:Okinawa Island|Okinawa Main Island]].<ref name=shimpo/><ref>高橋恵子『沖縄の御願ことば辞典』ボーダーインク, p.70</ref> The regions where Kamiashagi are found are classified as the "Kami-asagi Cultural Sphere" and the "Kitayama Cultural Sphere," and are said to overlap with the territory of the [[:en:Hokuzan|Kitayama Kingdom]] prior to the unification of the Three Mountains.<ref>高橋哲朗「沖縄の伝統行事 芸能を歩く」沖縄探見社, p. 59</ref>

== Cultural Properties == The following Kamiashagi have been designated as {{ill|Tangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|有形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Okinawa Prefecture: * [[:en:Iheya, Okinawa|Iheiya Village]] – the Kamiashagi of Gakiya, 1 structure – designated on July 11, 1977 (Showa 52)<ref name=list>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.okinawa.jp/edu/bunkazai/edu/jimukyoku/bunkazai/documents/h25_012.pdf |title=沖縄県教育庁文化財課要覧（平成28th year 度版） 国・県・市町村指定文化財|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-02-08}}</ref> * [[:en:Iheya, Okinawa|Iheiya Village]] – the Kamiashagi of Shima-jiri, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * [[:en:Izena, Okinawa|Izena Village]] – the Kamiasagi Worship Hall at Shomimi, 1 structure – designated on February 4, 1994 (Heisei 6)<ref name=list/> * [[:en:Izena, Okinawa|Izena Village]] – the Kamiasagi annex residential site at Nakada, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * [[:en:Izena, Okinawa|Izena Village]] – the Kamiasagi Worship Hall at Izena, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * [[:en:Izena, Okinawa|Izena Village]] – the Kamiasagi annex residential site at Serikyaku, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カミアシャギ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カミアシャギ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カミアシャギ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カミアシャギ]]
'''Kamiashagi''' (神アシャギ, 神アサギ) is a type of {{ill|Hut|en|Hut|WD=Q5784097|lt=hut}} used to conduct community deity festivals in the northern region of {{ill|Okinawa Island|en|Okinawa Island|WD=Q600614|lt=Okinawa Main Island}}. These structures are characterized by an open interior with no {{ill|Wall|en|Wall|WD=Q42948|lt=wall}}, supported by either four or six pillars, lacking a floor structure, with extremely low eaves that require one to stoop in order to enter. The traditional style features a thatched {{ill|Roof|en|Roof|WD=Q83180|lt=roof}} in the form of a {{ill|Hip roof|en|Hip roof|WD=Q162034|lt=hip roof}}, though in the postwar period, concrete constructions ({{ill|Concrete|en|Concrete|WD=Q22657|lt=concrete}}) have also appeared.<ref name=shimpo>[https://ryukyushimpo.jp/okinawa-dic/prentry-40953.html 最新版沖縄コンパクト事典]</ref>
[[File:Kami-Asagi.jpg|thumb|Kamiashagi|250px]]
== Overview == Kamiashagi is considered an ancient form of the shrine hall (tun) used as a venue for ritual ceremonies in central-southern {{ill|Okinawa Island|en|Okinawa Island|WD=Q600614|lt=Okinawa Main Island}}.<ref name=shimpo/><ref>高橋恵子『沖縄の御願ことば辞典』ボーダーインク, p.70</ref> The regions where Kamiashagi are found are classified as the "Kami-asagi Cultural Sphere" and the "Kitayama Cultural Sphere," and are said to overlap with the territory of the {{ill|Hokuzan|en|Hokuzan|WD=Q55523|lt=Kitayama Kingdom}} prior to the unification of the Three Mountains.<ref>高橋哲朗「沖縄の伝統行事 芸能を歩く」沖縄探見社, p. 59</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The following Kamiashagi have been designated as {{ill|Tangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|有形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Okinawa Prefecture: * {{ill|Iheya, Okinawa|en|Iheya, Okinawa|WD=Q1193315|lt=Iheiya Village}} – the Kamiashagi of Gakiya, 1 structure – designated on July 11, 1977 (Showa 52)<ref name=list>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.okinawa.jp/edu/bunkazai/edu/jimukyoku/bunkazai/documents/h25_012.pdf |title=沖縄県教育庁文化財課要覧（平成28th year 度版） 国・県・市町村指定文化財|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-02-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Iheya, Okinawa|en|Iheya, Okinawa|WD=Q1193315|lt=Iheiya Village}} – the Kamiashagi of Shima-jiri, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena, Okinawa|en|Izena, Okinawa|WD=Q981332|lt=Izena Village}} – the Kamiasagi Worship Hall at Shomimi, 1 structure – designated on February 4, 1994 (Heisei 6)<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena, Okinawa|en|Izena, Okinawa|WD=Q981332|lt=Izena Village}} – the Kamiasagi annex residential site at Nakada, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena, Okinawa|en|Izena, Okinawa|WD=Q981332|lt=Izena Village}} – the Kamiasagi Worship Hall at Izena, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena, Okinawa|en|Izena, Okinawa|WD=Q981332|lt=Izena Village}} – the Kamiasagi annex residential site at Serikyaku, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/>

[[File:Kami-Asagi.jpg|thumb|Kamiashagi|250px]]
== Overview == Kamiashagi is considered an ancient form of the shrine hall (tun) used as a venue for ritual ceremonies in central-southern {{ill|Okinawa Island|en|Okinawa Island|WD=Q600614|lt=Okinawa Main Island|1=Okinawa Island}}.<ref name=shimpo/><ref>高橋恵子『沖縄の御願ことば辞典』ボーダーインク, p.70</ref> The regions where Kamiashagi are found are classified as the "Kami-asagi Cultural Sphere" and the "Kitayama Cultural Sphere," and are said to overlap with the territory of the {{ill|Hokuzan|en|Hokuzan|WD=Q55523|lt=Kitayama Kingdom|1=Hokuzan}} prior to the unification of the Three Mountains.<ref>高橋哲朗「沖縄の伝統行事 芸能を歩く」沖縄探見社, p. 59</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The following Kamiashagi have been designated as {{ill|Tangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|有形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Okinawa Prefecture: * {{ill|Iheya, Okinawa|en|Iheya, Okinawa|WD=Q1193315|lt=Iheiya Village|1=Iheya, Okinawa}} – the Kamiashagi of Gakiya, 1 structure – designated on July 11, 1977 (Showa 52)<ref name=list>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.okinawa.jp/edu/bunkazai/edu/jimukyoku/bunkazai/documents/h25_012.pdf |title=沖縄県教育庁文化財課要覧（平成28th year 度版） 国・県・市町村指定文化財|format=PDF|accessdate=2018-02-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Iheya, Okinawa|en|Iheya, Okinawa|WD=Q1193315|lt=Iheiya Village|1=Iheya, Okinawa}} – the Kamiashagi of Shima-jiri, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena, Okinawa|en|Izena, Okinawa|WD=Q981332|lt=Izena Village|1=Izena, Okinawa}} – the Kamiasagi Worship Hall at Shomimi, 1 structure – designated on February 4, 1994 (Heisei 6)<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena, Okinawa|en|Izena, Okinawa|WD=Q981332|lt=Izena Village|1=Izena, Okinawa}} – the Kamiasagi annex residential site at Nakada, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena, Okinawa|en|Izena, Okinawa|WD=Q981332|lt=Izena Village|1=Izena, Okinawa}} – the Kamiasagi Worship Hall at Izena, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/> * {{ill|Izena, Okinawa|en|Izena, Okinawa|WD=Q981332|lt=Izena Village|1=Izena, Okinawa}} – the Kamiasagi annex residential site at Serikyaku, 1 structure – designated as above<ref name=list/>

[[ja:カミアシャギ]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q48764287}} [[Category:Religion in the Ryukyu Islands]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カミアシャギ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カミアシャギ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カミアシャギ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カミアシャギ]]
'''Kamiashagi''' (神アシャギ, 神アサギ) is a type of {{ill|Hut|en|Hut|qid=Q5784097|lt=hut|1=Hut}} used to conduct community deity festivals in the northern region of {{ill|Okinawa Island|en|Okinawa Island|qid=Q600614|lt=Okinawa Main Island|1=Okinawa Island}}. These structures are characterized by an open interior with no {{ill|Wall|en|Wall|qid=Q42948|lt=wall|1=Wall}}, supported by either four or six pillars, lacking a floor structure, with extremely low eaves that require one to stoop in order to enter. The traditional style features a thatched {{ill|Roof|en|Roof|qid=Q83180|lt=roof|1=Roof}} in the form of a {{ill|Hip roof|en|Hip roof|qid=Q162034|lt=hip roof|1=Hip roof}}, though in the postwar period, concrete constructions ({{ill|Concrete|en|Concrete|qid=Q22657|lt=concrete|1=Concrete}}) have also appeared.<ref name=shimpo>[https://ryukyushimpo.jp/okinawa-dic/prentry-40953.html 最新版沖縄コンパクト事典]</ref>
[[File:Kami-Asagi.jpg|thumb|Kamiashagi|250px]]
== Overview == Kamiashagi is considered an ancient form of the shrine hall (tun) used as a venue for ritual ceremonies in central-southern {{ill|Okinawa Island|en|Okinawa Island|qid=Q600614|lt=Okinawa Main Island|1=Okinawa Island}}.<ref name=shimpo/><ref>高橋恵子『沖縄の御願ことば辞典』ボーダーインク, p.70</ref> The regions where Kamiashagi are found are classified as the "Kami-asagi Cultural Sphere" and the "Kitayama Cultural Sphere," and are said to overlap with the territory of the {{ill|Hokuzan|en|Hokuzan|qid=Q55523|lt=Kitayama Kingdom|1=Hokuzan}} prior to the unification of the Three Mountains.<ref>高橋哲朗「沖縄の伝統行事 芸能を歩く」沖縄探見社, p. 59</ref>
